statement.p: Evaluate the following and express your answer as a single fraction.

p.em: You might want to do a preview before submitting your answer to be certain it has been entered correctly.

statement.p: Simplify the expression \frac{x^2+7 x+10}{x^2+7 x+6}\cdot \frac{x^2+4 x+3}{x^2+8 x+12} and give your answer in the form of \frac{f(x)}{g(x)}.
Your answer for the function f(x) is :
Your answer for the function g(x) is :

p.em: You might want to do a preview before submitting your answer to be certain it has been entered correctly.

statement.p: Evaluate the following and express your answer as a single fraction.

p.em: You might want to do a preview before submitting your answer to be certain it has been entered correctly.

hint.p: First you will need to get a common denominator by finding the least common multiple.
The common denominator is x y but you will need to put parenthesis around the denominator so that you divide it all.

statement.p: Find the least common denominator (LCD) of the rational expressions: \frac{2y}{y^{2}-25} \mbox{ and } \frac{4}{y^{2}-5y}

solution.p: The factors of the denominators are
Hence, the LCD is y\mathopen{}\left(y-5\right)\mathopen{}\left(y+5\right).

statement.p: Simplify the expression \frac{\frac{2}{a+h}-\frac{2}{a}}{h} and give your answer in the form of \frac{A}{B}.
Simplify the expression \frac{1+\frac{2}{c-2}}{1-\frac{2}{c-2}} and give your answer in the form of \frac{f(c)}{g(c)}.
Your answer for the function f(c) is :
Your answer for the function g(c) is :
Your answer for the function f(x) is :
Your answer for the function g(x) is :
Multiply the two rational expressions \frac{9 x^{2}}{35 y^{2}} \cdot \frac{15 xy}{3 x^{2}y^2}. Express your answer in simplest form \frac{A}{B}.

li.p: None of the above

statement.p: Divide the two rational expressions - \frac{30 a b^{6}}{7 c} \div - \frac{3 a^2 c}{b^{3}c^{6}}. Express your answer in simplest form \frac{A}{B}.
The answer has the form of \displaystyle{\ \frac{f(x)}{g(x)}\ } where
Perform the indicated operations on the three expressions x + \frac{7}{x^{2}-49} - \frac{x^{2}}{x+7}. Express your answer in simplest form \frac{A}{B}.
Perform the indicated operations involving the three rational expressions \frac{6xy^{4}}{3x} \cdot \frac{6xy^{6}}{26y} \div \frac{3y^{2}z}{13x^{3}}. Express your answer in simplest form \frac{A}{B}.
Simplify the expression \frac{\frac{4}{x-1}-\frac{1}{x+1}}{\frac{x}{x-1}+\frac{1}{x+1}} and give your answer in the form of \frac{f(x)}{g(x)}.

Your answer for the function g(x) is :

statement.p: Add the following and express your answer as a single fraction.

hint.p: First you will need to get a common denominator by finding the least common multiple.
The common denominator is x y but you will need to put parenthesis around the denominator so that you divide it all.

statement.p: The answer has the form of \displaystyle{\frac{f(x)}{g(x)}} where
Add the two rational expressions \frac{2x-1}{2x^{2}} + \frac{5\mathopen{}\left(x-2\right)}{2x^{2}}. Express your answer in simplest form \frac{A}{B}.
Simplify the expression \frac{2}{x^2} + \frac{4}{x^2+x} and give your answer in the form of \frac{f(x)}{g(x)}.
Your answer for the function f(x) is :
Your answer for the function g(x) is :
Subtract the two rational expressions \frac{7x}{3x+1} - \frac{5}{3x+1}. Express your answer in simplest form \frac{A}{B}.
Find the least common denominator (LCD) of the rational expressions: \frac{6t+1}{16t^{2}+24t+9} \mbox{ and } \frac{2t+3}{16t^{2}-9}

solution.p: The factors of the denominators are
Hence, the LCD is \left(4t-3\right)\mathopen{}\left(4t+3\right)^{2}.

statement.p: Note: Your answer should be a completely simplified fraction. Unsimplified answers and decimals will not be accepted.
Perform the indicated operation. Simplify the result, if possible. 11 - \frac{5}{15x}
Select all expressions that are equivalent to \displaystyle y + \frac{y}{5} + \frac{5}{y}. There may be more than one correct answer.

(Not randomizable) Suppose you have used long division to divide the polynomial (7x^2 -3x + 5) by (x+1)\text{.} Suppose you found that the remainder is 15 and the quotient is 7x-10\text{.} Write the result in the format of (1.2.1).
Note: If the help buttons on the right side block part of the question, click somewhere else in the question to temporarily hide the buttons.

li.p:
Then x^2-1 is called the
?
dividend
divisor
quotient
remainder .

Then 4x^3-3x+7 is called the
?
dividend
divisor
quotient
remainder .
The quotient is
The remainder is

statement.p: (Not randomizable) Use long division to find the quotient and remainder when
(Not randomizable) Use long division to find the quotient and remainder when
(Not randomizable) Use long division to find the quotient and remainder when
(Not randomizable) Use long division to find the quotient and remainder when
Write the answer in the format of (1.2.1)
(Not randomizable) Which of the following statements are true and which are false?

li.p:
If the remainder is zero, then the divisor is a factor of the dividend.
?
True
False

If the remainder is NOT zero, then the divisor is NOT a factor of the dividend.
?
True
False

If a degree 6 polynomial is divided by a degree 2 polynomial, then the quotient is a degree 4 polynomial.
?
True
False

If a degree 3 polynomial is divided by a linear polynomial, then the quotient is a linear polynomial.
?
True
False

Here 3x^2-2x-85 is called the
?
dividend
divisor
quotient
remainder .

Here x+5 is called the
?
dividend
divisor
quotient
remainder .
Did Lennie set up the problem correctly?
Yes, it is a correct start
No, it is not a correct start
Now finish the long division and find the quotient and remainder (if there is one).

Remainder =

?
dividend

quotient
remainder .

divisor
quotient
remainder .

?
dividend
divisor
quotient
remainder .

Yes, it is correct
No, there is one error

statement.p: Note: If the help buttons on the right side block part of the question, click somewhere else in the question to temporarily hide the buttons.
Find the quotient and remainder using long division for
Use long division to find the quotient and remainder when f(x)= 5 x^4 - 25 x^3 - 20 x^2 - 35 x - 15 is divided by g(x)= 5 x - 6.
Use long division to find the quotient and remainder when f(x)=-1 x^5 + 9 x^4 + 3 x^3 + 5 x^2 - 4 x + 7 is divided by g(x)=5 x^2 - 1 x - 4.

Use the properties of exponents to simplify the following

solution.p: We add the exponents as follows

statement.p: Note: Your answer should be completely simplified. Unsimplified answers will not be accepted.
Simplify the following expression, and write your answer using only positive exponents.
Note that you may have to be careful with grouping parenthesis in this problem; 1/2t is not the same as 1/(2t).

solution.p: We use the properties of exponents to help us here, remembering that \displaystyle{\frac{a^m}{a^n} = a^{m-n}}

statement.p: Rewrite the following using a single exponent.
Evaluate the following without a calculator. Simplify your answers as much as possible.
Find the product ( -7 \sqrt{3})( 8 \sqrt{5} ) and express your answer in simplest radical form A \sqrt{C}, where A and C are integers.
Find the product \sqrt{3} \left( \sqrt{11} + \sqrt{7} \right) and express your answer in the form A \sqrt{B} + C \sqrt{D}, A, B, C, and D are all integers and B > D.
Answer: A = , B = , C = , and D =
Evaluate the following expression without using a calculator. Simplify your answer as much as possible, and enter your answer as a fraction.
Simplify and write the following in exponential notation.
If \left( t^{ \frac{2}{3} } \cdot p^{\frac{5}{9} } \right)^{\frac{2}{9}} = t^{m} \cdot p^n then m = and n =
Simplify. Assume that all expressions under radicals represent nonnegative numbers.
Write your answer using radical notation if necessary.
Evaluate the following expressions for x = 2, y = -4, and z = -3 without using a calculator. Simplify your answers as much as possible, and enter your answers as fractions.
Evaluate the following without a calculator. Simplify your answers as much as possible.
Simplify and write the following as a power of u with a single rational exponent.

solution.p: Recall that \displaystyle{\frac{x^a}{x^b} = x^{a-b}}
So, in this case we will need to subtract the exponents,
\displaystyle{ \frac{2}{7} - \frac{5}{6} = {-{\frac{23}{42}}}}.
Therefore,
which means m = {-{\frac{23}{42}}}.

statement.p: Simplify the expression as much as possible and leave it without radicals.

Answer: A = , B = , C = , and D =

solution.p: Recall the properties of exponents:

statement.p: Simplify the following expression (do not factor)

solution.p: We divide each term by {4x^{5}y^{2}} as follows

statement.p: Simplify each expression as much as possible and leave it without radicals.

p.em: You cannot use any operations except division (/) and negation (-).

statement.p: Note: Your answer should be completely simplified. Unsimplified answers will not be accepted.

solution.p: Use properties of exponents to simplify the left side of the equation\displaystyle{\begin{gathered} \frac{(4.1)^x (4.1)^{-2}}{(4.1)^{5}} = (4.1)^{8} \\ (4.1)^x (4.1)^{-2} (4.1)^{-5} = (4.1)^{8} \\ (4.1)^{(x - 2-5)} = (4.1)^{8}\ . \end{gathered} } The two sides are equal if and only if the exponents are equal, so \displaystyle{ x - 2-5 = 8 }. Solving for x one obtains \displaystyle{ x = {15} }.

statement.p: Combine radicals, if possible. Simplify your answer as much as possible.
Find the product ( 8 \sqrt[3]{6})( 3 \sqrt[3]{9} ) and express your answer in simplest radical form A \sqrt[3]{C}, where A and C are integers.
Simplify the expression 3 \sqrt[3]{2} \left( 4 \sqrt[3]{4} + 5 \sqrt[3]{7} \right) and express your answer in the simplest radical form A+ B \sqrt[3]{C}, where A, B, and C are integers.
For the given values of an angle \alpha, find the terminal point P(x,y) on the unit circle.

hint.p: For each given value of \alpha, draw a unit circle and the angle \alpha in standard position. The point desired is where the terminal side of the angle intersects the unit circle. Use geometric ideas to determine x and y in a manner similar to what was done in earlier sections of this course.

statement.p: Use the triangle in the image and evaluate the following trigonometric quanities. Answer with exact values.

li.p:
We know that \csc \theta = \frac{1}{\sin \theta}\text{.} Therefore, in terms of right angled triangles, \csc \theta = \frac{1}{opp/hyp} =
?
opposite
adjacent
hypothenuse /
?
opposite
adjacent
hypothenuse .

In right angled triangles, \sec \theta =
?
opposite
adjacent
hypothenuse /
?
opposite
adjacent
hypothenuse .

?
opposite
adjacent
hypothenuse /
?
opposite
adjacent
hypothenuse .

?
x
y
r /
?
x
y
r .

?
x
y
r /
?
x
y
r .

?

?
x

r .

statement.p: An angle \alpha in standard position has a terminal side illustrated below. Click on the picture for a larger version.
Using the grids provided, determine sufficiently accurate values for a point (x,y) and corresponding radius r. Using these values, determine reasonable approximations for the following:

solution.p: Pick any point, except the origin, on the terminal side of the angle. I'll pick (3,8). Use the Pythagorean Theorem to find r.
The distance from (3,8) to the origin is r=\sqrt{(3)^2+(8)^2} = \sqrt{73} \approx 8.544 Plug into the definition:

statement.p: Write the expression \csc x \tan x - 7\sec x in terms of only sine and cosine, and simplify as much as possible.

hint.p: Re-write each of the trigonometric functions in terms of \sin x and \cos x\text{,} then simplify. Remember that your final answer should not use any of the other trigonometric functions, only \sin x and/or \cos x\text{.}

statement.p: Use the graphs of trigonometric functions to find the following information.
(c) All values of x in the interval [-2\pi, 2\pi] where \sin x = -1\text{.}
Which of the following statements are true, and which are false? Enter 1 for TRUE and 0 for FALSE.
(a) y=\csc x is an odd function.
Compared to the graph of y=\tan x\text{,} the graph of y=\tan (2x) is compressed horizontally by a factor of 2\text{.}
The graph below illustrates one complete cycle for y = A\cos(Bx+c) with A positive.

p.em: Note: Use a negative for a shift to the left.

hint.p: The normal period for the tangent curve is \pi. For a curve of the form \tan(Bx) the period is found using the formula \pi/B.
To determine the appropriate phase shift, it is often easier to determine by factoring \tan(Bx+C) = \tan(B(x+\frac{C}{B}).

statement.p: (a) Sketch a few periods of the graph of y=\sin x\text{.}
(b) Sketch a few periods of the graph of y=3\cos x\text{.}
(c) Sketch a few periods of the graph of y=\tan (x-\pi)\text{.}
(d) It is true or false that \tan (x-\pi) = \tan x\text{?} (Enter 1 for TRUE and 0 for FALSE.)
(e) What is the period of y=5 \sin (5 x-9)\text{?}
Find the following for the function y=6\cos \left(2x\right)
(a) What is the period of y=6\sin \left(2x+4\right)\text{?}
(b) What is the amplitude of y=6\sin \left(2x+4\right)\text{?}
(c) What is the phase shift of y=6\sin \left(2x+4\right)\text{?} (Use a positive number for a shift to the right, and negative for to the left.)
Find the following for the function y=7\sin \left(7x-8\right)
(a) What is the period of y=7\sin \left(7x+8\right)\text{?}
(b) What is the amplitude of y=7\sin \left(7x+8\right)\text{?}
(c) What is the phase shift of y=7\sin \left(7x+8\right)\text{?} (Use a positive number for a shift to the right, and negative for to the left.)
Which function is described by the plot below?

li.p: none of the above

statement.p: Below is the graph of the function f(x) = 10 \sin{ \left( \frac{\pi}{5} x \right)} in blue, and a second sinusoidal function y = g(x) in red, which is a horizontal shift of y = f(x). Find a formula for the function g(x).

static.solution:
SOLUTION
Since graph has been shifted by \frac{ 4 }{10} of a period, the phase shift is \phi = (2 \pi ) \cdot \left( \frac{ 4 }{10} \right) = \frac{4 \pi}{5}. Therefore the formula for g(x) is: g(x) = 10 \sin{ \left( \frac{\pi}{5} \ x - \phi \right) } = 10 \sin{ \left( \frac{\pi}{5} \ x - \frac{4 \pi}{5} \right) }.

statement.p: Find a formula for the trigonometric function graphed below. Use x as the independent variable in your formula.

static.solution:
SOLUTION
This function resembles a cosine curve in that it attains its maximum when x = 0. We know that the smallest value it attains is -0.8, that its midline is y = 0, so its amplitude is 0.8. Thus in the equation f(x) = A \cos{(Bx)} + k we know that A = 0.8 and k = 0. Since the graph has a period of \frac{\pi}{14}, the value for B = \frac{2 \pi}{\mbox{period}} = \frac{2 \pi}{\pi/14} = 2 \cdot 14 = 28. Finally we have a formula: f(x) = 0.8 \cos{(28 x)}.

statement.p: Find a formula for the trigonometric function graphed below. Use x as the independent variable in your formula.

static.solution:
SOLUTION
This function resembles a cosine curve in that it attains its minimum (not midline) when x = 0. Since the value is at its minimum (not maximum) at x = 0, we need to use the formula f(x) = - \cos{(x)}. We know that the smallest value it attains is 0, that its midline is y = 3, so its amplitude is 3 as well. Thus in the equation f(x) = -A \cos{(Bx)} + k we know that A = 3 and k = 3. Since the graph has a period of 10, the value for B = \frac{2 \pi}{\mbox{period}} = \frac{2 \pi}{10} = \frac{\pi}{5}. Finally we have a formula: f(x) = 3-3 \cos{ \left( \frac{\pi}{5} x \right) }.

statement.p: Suppose y = 6 \cos{ (4 t + 28 )} - 4. In your answers, enter pi for \pi.

static.solution:
SOLUTION
(a) We see that the phase shift is -28, since the function is in a form that shows it.
(b) To find the horizontal shift, we factor out a 4 within the cosine function, giving us y = 6 \cos{ (4 t + 28 )} - 4 = 6 \cos{ \big( 4 (t + 7) \big) } - 4 Thus, the horizontal shift is -7.

statement.p: Suppose y = -3 \cos{ (5 t + 20 )} + 5. In your answers, enter pi for \pi.

static.solution:
SOLUTION
To find the horizontal shift, we factor out a 5 within the cosine function, giving us y = -3 \cos{ (5 t + 20 )} + 5 = -3 \cos{ \big( 5 (t + 4) \big) } + 5 Thus, the horizontal shift is -4.

statement.p: Without a calculator, sketch the first two periods of the graph of the function f(x) = -1 - 4 \cos{( 70 \pi x)}.

Which graph below A-E matches your sketch?
B
D
Choose
E
A
C
(Click on a graph to enlarge it)

static.solution:
SOLUTION
The function f(x) = -1 - 4 \cos{( 70 \pi x)} has midline y = k = -1 and amplitude | A | = 4. Since B = 70 \pi, the period is \mbox{period} = \frac{ 2 \pi}{B} = \frac{2 \pi}{70 \pi} = \frac{1}{35}. Since this is a cosine function with a negative value for the parameter A = - 4, when x = 0 the graph is at its minimum value. Therefore the correct answer is graph C .

statement.p: Decide whether the following graph appears to be a periodic function. If so enter the value of its period in the blank. If the graph does not appear to be periodic enter NONE.
The period is (Enter NONE if not periodic.)

static.solution:
SOLUTION
This is not the graph of a periodic function. Although the y-values repeat, they do not repeat at regular intervals. Moving away from the origin horizontally, the intervals become shorter and shorter.

statement.p: Decide whether the following graph appears to be a periodic function. If so enter the value of its period in the blank. If the graph does not appear to be periodic enter NONE.
The period is (Enter NONE if not periodic.)

static.solution:
SOLUTION
This appears to be a periodic function. It repeats regularly with period 3.

statement.p: Use the special triangles to find the following values. Answer in exact form (using square roots if needed).
Use the special triangles to find the following values. Answer in exact form (using square roots if needed).
Use the special triangles to find the following values. Answer in exact form (using square roots if needed).

Evaluate the following. Answer in exact form (using square roots if needed).
Evaluate the following. Answer in exact form (using square roots if needed).

\cot \left({2.5}\pi\right) = .
\sin \left({2.5}\pi\right) = .
\csc \left({2.5}\pi\right) = .
\cos \left({2.5}\pi\right) = .

statement.p: Evaluate the following without using a calculator. Use one of the techniques discussed in this section, as appropriate.
Evaluate the following without using a calculator. Use one of the techniques discussed in this section, as appropriate.
Evaluate the following without using a calculator. Use one of the techniques discussed in this section, as appropriate.
Evaluate the following without using a calculator. Use one of the techniques discussed in this section, as appropriate.
If an answer is undefined, input undefined .
Evaluate the following without using a calculator. Use one of the techniques discussed in this section, as appropriate.
Evaluate the following without using a calculator. Use one of the techniques discussed in this section, as appropriate.
Evaluate the following without using a calculator. Use one of the techniques discussed in this section, as appropriate.
Evaluate the following without using a calculator. Use one of the techniques discussed in this section, as appropriate.
Evaluate \arcsin \left(-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value.
Evaluate \arcsin \left(\frac{1}{2}\right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value.
Evaluate \sin \left(\arcsin \left(-\frac{{5}}{{6}}\right)\right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value.
Evaluate \arcsin \left(\sin \left(\frac{5\pi}{6}\right)\right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value.
Evaluate \arccos \left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value.
Evaluate \arccos 1 without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value.
Evaluate \cos \left(\arccos \left(\frac{{10}}{{11}}\right)\right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value.
Evaluate \arccos \left(\cos \left(-\frac{\pi}{{12}}\right)\right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value.
Evaluate \arctan 1 without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value.
Evaluate \mbox{arcsec} \left(-\sqrt{2} \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value.
Evaluate \mbox{arccsc} \left(\sqrt{2} \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value.
State the domain and range of each of the following functions:
(Enter your answers as inequalities: . Your domains should be inequalities in x and ranges, in y. As always, type pi for \pi. If you wish to enter \infty, type infinity.)

static.solution:
SOLUTION
(a) The domain of f is -1 \le x \le 1, so \arccos{(x)} is defined only if x is between -1 and 1 inclusive. The range is between 0 and \pi inclusive.
(b) The domain of g is -1 \le x \le 1, so \arcsin{(x)} is defined only if x is between -1 and 1 inclusive. The range is between -\frac{\pi}{2} and \frac{\pi}{2} inclusive.
(c) The domain of h is all real numbers, so \arctan{(x)} is defined for all values of x. The range is between but not including -\frac{\pi}{2} and \frac{\pi}{2}.

statement.p: Evaluate \arcsin \left(-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \arcsin \left(\frac{1}{2} \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \arccos \left(\frac{1}{2} \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \cos^{-1} \left(-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \arctan \left(-1 \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \arcsin \left(-1 \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \sin^{-1} \left(0 \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \arccos \left(-1 \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \arcsin \left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \arctan \left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \tan^{-1} \left(-\sqrt{3} \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \mbox{arccot} \left(\sqrt{3} \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \mbox{arccot} \left(0 \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \mbox{arcsec} \left(-1 \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \mbox{arccsc} \left(-\sqrt{2} \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \mbox{arcsec} \left(\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \arcsin \left( \sin \left(\frac{5\pi}{12} \right) \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \arcsin \left( \sin \left(-\frac{7\pi}{6} \right) \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \arccos \left( \cos \left(\frac{4\pi}{3} \right) \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \arccos \left( \cos \left(\frac{4\pi}{3} \right) \right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value, in radians.
Evaluate \tan \left(\arctan \left(\frac{{8}}{{9}}\right)\right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value.
Evaluate \cos \left(2 \arcsin \left(1\right)\right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value.
Evaluate \tan \left(4 \cos^{-1} \left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)\right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value.
Evaluate \sec \left(\arccos \left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right) - 6\arcsin \left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)\right) without using a calculator. Answer with an exact value.
Use the properties of exponents to simplify the following

solution.p: We add the exponents as follows

statement.p: Use the properties of exponents to simplify the following

solution.p: We add the exponents as follows

statement.p: Suppose \displaystyle \sin{ \theta } = \frac{x}{5} and the angle \theta is in the first quadrant. Write algebraic expressions for \cos( \theta ) and \tan( \theta ) in terms of x.
Suppose \displaystyle \sin{ \theta } = \frac{x}{6} and the angle \theta is in the first quadrant. Write algebraic expressions for \cos( \theta ) and \tan( \theta ) in terms of x.
Find a formula for the inverse of the function \displaystyle g(x) = \frac{e^x}{7+4 e^x}.
Write the expression 6 \csc \theta \tan \theta - 2\sec \theta in terms of only sine and cosine, and simplify as much as possible.
Use the special triangles to find the following values. Answer in exact form (using square roots if needed).

Consider the function f defined by f(x)={\sqrt{x}}\text{.}
The exact value of f(12) is and a decimal approximation for this is .
The domain of this function, in interval notation, is .
The graph of y={\sqrt{x}} intersects the graph of y=6-x at .
\frac{d}{dx}{\sqrt{x}}={} .
The formula for f(x)^2\text{,} including its restricted domain, is .
f is a
?
power
exponential
linear
quadratic function.
Which is true of the word radical?
It shares ancestry with "radius", as in the radius of a circle.
It shares ancestry with "radish", a vegetable.
Math mode does not seem to work within radio buttons.
